Context: Texas traffic stop · Your rights auto-loaded · 467 protections available
🤖
Hello! I'm your C.A.R.E.N. Legal Assistant. I've loaded your Texas legal rights profile. How can I help you today? I can coach you through police encounters, explain your rights, or help you find an attorney.
9:38 AM
I'm being pulled over right now. What do I say?
9:40 AM
🤖
Stay calm. Here's exactly what to say and do right now:

1. Pull over safely, turn off engine, hands on wheel
2. When officer approaches, say this script:
🗣️ Say This Now
"Officer, I want to be cooperative. Am I being detained or am I free to go? I am invoking my right to remain silent and I do not consent to any searches."
You only need to provide your driver's license, registration, and proof of insurance in Texas.
9:40 AM
They're asking me to step out of the car
9:41 AM
🤖
Comply with the request to exit — Pennsylvania v. Mimms gives officers this right during any traffic stop. Exit calmly. Then say:
🗣️ Next Script
"I am complying. I do not consent to any searches. I am invoking my right to remain silent. I would like to speak with an attorney."
9:41 AM
🚨 Activate SOS
👨‍⚖️ Find Attorney
📹 Start Recording
Can they search my car?
🎤
Ask a legal question...
